title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mipdeval R package evaluating predictive performance PK/PD models historical datasets model-informed precision dosing (MIPD). iteratively loops NONMEM-style datasets, performing MAP Bayesian updating forecasting step—similar PsN’s proseval tool, MIPD-specific features like prior flattening, time-based weighting, sample grouping. Core dependencies: - PKPDmap (InsightRX/PKPDmap): MAP Bayesian fitting via get_map_estimates() - PKPDsim (InsightRX/PKPDsim): PK/PD model objects regimen handling

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Load package for development devtools::load_all()  # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a single test file devtools::test(filter = "run_eval")  # Regenerate documentation (required after changing roxygen2 comments) devtools::document()  # Full package check devtools::check()  ## Create documentation devtools::document()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: main entry point run_eval() (R/run_eval.R), orchestrates: Model parsing (parse_model.R): S3 dispatch handles PKPDsim objects, character strings (model library names), PKPDsim object types. Extracts parameters, omega, ruv, fixed effects, IOV bins. Data parsing (parse_input_data.R): Splits NONMEM-style data subject ID, creates regimen/observation/covariate structures, applies grouping logic. Core iterative loop (run_eval_core.R): Called per-subject (parallelized via furrr). iterative observation group: applies sample weights, censors future covariates prevent data leakage, calls PKPDmap::get_map_estimates(), generates population/individual/iterative predictions. Statistics (calculate_stats.R, calculate_shrinkage.R, calculate_bayesian_impact.R): Computes RMSE, NRMSE, MAPE, MPE, accuracy, shrinkage, Bayesian impact metrics. Failed fits (surface NA predictions) detected reported check_failed_fits.R. run_eval() calls per-subject loop emit single warning; calculate_stats() also calls (controllable via warn argument, run_eval() sets FALSE avoid duplicate warning).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: run_eval() returns list class "mipdeval_results": - results: Full prediction parameter data - mod_obj: Parsed model information - data: Input dataset - sim: VPC simulations (requested) - stats_summ: Summary statistics - shrinkage: Shrinkage metrics - bayesian_impact: Bayesian updating impact

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Configuration helpers: vpc_options(), fit_options(), stats_summ_options() return typed option lists—use instead raw lists arguments. Bootstrap settings (bootstrap_options()) nested inside stats_summ_options(bootstrap = ...), sharing accuracy error margins. Sample weighting (calculate_fit_weights.R): Multiple schemes (weight_all, weight_last_only, weight_last_two_only, weight_gradient_linear, weight_gradient_exponential). Grouping columns (add_grouping_column.R): Group observations dose time range group_by_dose() / group_by_time(). Parallelism: run.R wraps purrr::map() optional furrr parallelism.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Tests use testthat edition 3. tests/testthat/setup.R installs required PKPDsim model libraries (pkvancothomson, pkvbusulfanshukla) tests run. Visual regression tests use vdiffr. Reference PsN proseval results live inst/extdata/vanco_thomson.csv used validate results within ~10% tolerance.

what: Evaluate iterative predictive performance of an installed model
title: mipdeval
text: run_eval() nice way evaluate iterative predictive performance PKPDsim model using NONMEM-style dataset, performing MAP Bayesian updating forecasting step. run_eval() can evaluate -memory PKPDsim model objects installed PKPDsim model libraries (see vignette("PKPDsim", package = "PKPDsim") overview different model formats). evaluate predictive performance "pkvancothomson" model, packaged within PKPDsim can installed model library : example use nm_vanco data set, NONMEM-style data frame 80 rows 11 columns: ID: Patient ID. TIME: recorded times dosing events /observations CMT: Dose compartment. DV: dependent variable observed concentration. AMT: Dose administered dosing record. EVID: Event ID (0 = observation, 1 = dose, 2 = , 3 = reset, 4 = reset + dose). MDV: Missing DV (0 = missing, 1 = missing). RATE: Rate drug infusion. WT: Patient weight TIME measurement. CRCL: Patient creatinine clearance TIME measurement. CL_HEMO: Additional clearance attributable hemodialysis. predictive performance model can evaluated using run_eval(). using installed PKPDsim model library, model argument simply requires name library.

what: Evaluate iterative predictive performance of an in-memory model
title: mipdeval
text: evaluating -memory PKPDsim model objects, run_eval() requires several additional arguments addition model object: parameters: Named list model parameters. ruv: Named list specifying proportional /additive residual error variability magnitude. omega: -subject variability, supplied vector specifying lower triangle covariance matrix random effects. replicate model previous example hand: predictive performance model can evaluated using run_eval():
code: mod <- new_ode_model(   code = ("     dAdt[0] = -(CLtot/Vi)*A[0] - (Q/Vi)*A[0] + (Q/V2i)*A[1]     dAdt[1] = +(Q/Vi)*A[0] - (Q/V2i)*A[1]     dAdt[2] = A[0]/Vi   "),   pk_code = ("     CLi = CL * (1 + TH_CRCL * (CRCL*16.66667 - 66))     CLtot = CLi + CL_HEMO     Qi = Q     Vi = V * WT     V2i = V2 * WT   "),   covariates = c("WT", "CRCL", "CL_HEMO"),   declare_variables = c("CLi", "Qi", "Vi", "V2i", "CLtot"),   fixed = "TH_CRCL",   obs = list(scale = "V * WT") ) parameters <- list(CL = 2.99, V = 0.675, TH_CRCL = 0.0154, Q = 2.28, V2 = 0.732) ruv <- list(prop = 0.15, add = 1.6) omega <- c(0.0729, 0.0100, 0.0225, 0, 0, 0.2401, 0, 0, 0, 1.6900) run_eval(   model = mod,   data = nm_vanco,   parameters = parameters,   omega = omega,   ruv = ruv )

title: mipdeval
text: run_eval() performs similarly execute proseval tools Perl-speaks-NONMEM (PsN), offering following advantages: Ease use: Evaluates priori posteriori predictive performance one call. Tailored MIPD: MIPD-focused analysis options flattening priors, time-based weighting, sample grouping, etc. goal provide comparable (iterative) predictions mipdeval PsN. compare_psn_*_results() family functions can used calculate relative difference predictions run_eval() PsN execute proseval tools. compare execute results, use compare_psn_execute_results(): compare proseval results, use compare_psn_proseval_results(): underlying relative difference (iterative) prediction can also returned reldiff_psn_execute_results() reldiff_psn_proseval_results().

title: Accuracy — accuracy
text: Accuracy provides measure clinical suitability, defined whether model predicted drug concentrations fall within absolute relative error margin measured concentrations.

title: Accuracy — accuracy
code: accuracy(obs, pred, error_abs = 0, error_rel = 0)  is_accurate(obs, pred, error_abs = 0, error_rel = 0)  is_accurate_abs(obs, pred, error_abs = 0)  is_accurate_rel(obs, pred, error_rel = 0)

title: Accuracy — accuracy
text: obs Observations vector. pred Predictions vector. error_abs, error_rel Positive number providing absolute relative error margin. cutoff exclusive error margin. Defaults 0, meaning predictions fall within error margin.

title: Accuracy — accuracy
text: is_accurate(), is_accurate_abs(), is_accurate_rel(): logical vector indicating whether predicted drug concentration considered accurate according specified absolute relative error margin(s). accuracy(): single value 0 1 indicating proportion predicted drug concentrations fell within specified absolute relative error margins.

title: Accuracy — accuracy
code: # Does the predicted drug concentration fall within 0.5 mg/L error margin? is_accurate_abs(6, 5, error_abs = 0.5) #> [1] FALSE  # Does the predicted drug concentration fall within 25% error margin? is_accurate_rel(6, 5, error_rel = 0.25) #> [1] TRUE  # Does the predicted drug concentration fall within 0.5 mg/L OR 25%? is_accurate(6, 5, error_abs = 0.5, error_rel = 0.25) #> [1] TRUE  # What proportion of predicted drug concentrations fell within 0.5 mg/L OR 25%? accuracy(rnorm(10, 6), rnorm(10, 5), error_abs = 0.5, error_rel = 0.25) #> [1] 0.5

code: bootstrap_metrics(.data, ..., .by = NULL, .seed = 10, .n_boots = 1000)  summarise_bootstrap_metrics(.data, .by = NULL, .conf_level = 0.95)  summarize_bootstrap_metrics(.data, .by = NULL, .conf_level = 0.95)

title: Calculate metrics across bootstrapped folds — bootstrap_metrics
text: .data data frame data frame extension (e.g. tibble). ... <data-masking> Name-value pairs summary functions compute across bootstrapped folds data. name name variable result. .<tidy-select> Optionally, variables group . group resampled independently. .seed Single value random seed, used reproducible random sampling. .n_boots Number bootstrapped samples create (per group). .conf_level confidence level use confidence interval. Must strictly 0 1. Defaults 95 percent confidence interval.

title: Calculate metrics across bootstrapped folds — bootstrap_metrics
text: bootstrap_metrics(): tibble::tibble() whose columns combination summary expressions grouping keys provide, plus boot column indicating bootstrap fold. summarise_bootstrap_metrics(): tibble::tibble() , bootstrapped metric, three columns suffixed _mid, _lower, _upper, giving mean lower upper quantiles implied .conf_level, respectively.

title: Calculate metrics across bootstrapped folds — bootstrap_metrics
text: (Normalized) Root mean squared error: rmse(), nrmse() Mean (absolute) percent error: mpe(), mape() Accuracy: accuracy()

title: Calculate metrics across bootstrapped folds — bootstrap_metrics
code: set.seed(99) df <- data.frame(   observationid = rep(1:1000, 4),   model = rep(c(rep("A", 1000), rep("B", 1000)), 2),   patient_type = "general",   prediction_type = c(rep("a priori", 2000), rep("a posteriori", 2000)),   res = c(     rnorm(1000, 2, 3),     rnorm(1000, 0.1, 1),     rnorm(1000, 1, 3),     rnorm(1000, 0, 0.5)   ),   tdm = rnorm(4000, 10, 5) )  boots <- bootstrap_metrics(   df,   rmse = rmse(tdm, tdm - res),   nrmse = nrmse(tdm, tdm - res),   accuracy = accuracy(tdm, tdm - res, 2.5, 0.2),   .by = c(model, patient_type, prediction_type),   .n_boots = 100 )  summarise_bootstrap_metrics(   boots,   .by = c(model, patient_type, prediction_type) ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 12 #>   model patient_type prediction_type rmse_mid rmse_lower rmse_upper nrmse_mid #>   <chr> <chr>        <chr>              <dbl>      <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 A     general      a posteriori       3.21       3.07       3.37     0.326  #> 2 A     general      a priori           3.61       3.46       3.76     0.362  #> 3 B     general      a posteriori       0.490      0.469      0.506    0.0487 #> 4 B     general      a priori           0.986      0.945      1.04     0.0972 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: nrmse_lower <dbl>, nrmse_upper <dbl>, accuracy_mid <dbl>, #> #   accuracy_lower <dbl>, accuracy_upper <dbl>

what: mipdeval: Run evaluation of iterative predictive performance based on historical datasets — mipdeval-package
title: mipdeval: Run evaluation of iterative predictive performance based on historical datasets — mipdeval-package
text: package provides functionality run NONMEM-style dataset iteratively, performing MAP Bayesian updating forecasting step. much like `proseval` tool PsN, however package provides options, specifically use MIPD, flattening priors, time-based weighting, sample grouping, etc.

title: mipdeval: Run evaluation of iterative predictive performance based on historical datasets — mipdeval-package

title: Busulfan data — nm_busulfan
Busulfan data — nm_busulfan
text: NONMEM-style data frame 240 rows 13 columns: ID Patient ID. TIME recorded times dosing events /observations. CMT Dose compartment. DV dependent variable observed concentration. AMT Dose administered dosing record. EVID Event ID (0 = observation, 1 = dose, 2 = , 3 = reset, 4 = reset + dose). MDV Missing DV (0 = missing, 1 = missing). RATE Rate drug infusion. AGE Patient age TIME measurement. WT Patient weight TIME measurement. HT Patient height TIME measurement. SEX Patient sex (0 = female, 1 = male). REGI Regimen type.

title: Busulfan data — nm_busulfan
text: Shukla et al. Front Pharmacol. 2020. https://doi.org/10.3389/fphar.2020.00888

title: Run iterative predictive analysis, looping over each individual's data — run_eval
code: run_eval(   model,   data,   ids = NULL,   parameters = NULL,   fixed = NULL,   omega = NULL,   iov = NULL,   ruv = NULL,   dictionary = list(),   group = NULL,   weights = NULL,   weight_prior = 1,   censor_covariates = TRUE,   incremental = FALSE,   .stats_summ_options = stats_summ_options(),   .vpc_options = vpc_options(),   .fit_options = fit_options(),   threads = 1,   progress = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run iterative predictive analysis, looping over each individual's data — run_eval
text: model either PKPDsim model object, string pointing PKPDsim-generated model library, e.g. pkvancothomson data NONMEM-style data.frame, path CSV file NONMEM data ids optional, vector subject IDs run analysis (default runs analysis subjects dataset) parameters list parameters fixed fix specific parameters, supplied vector strings omega subject variability, supplied vector specifiying lower triangle covariance matrix random effects iov list specifying required metadata implementation IOV, specifically coefficient variation (CV %) IOV parameter vector bin separators. example, list(cv = list(CL = 0.1, V = 0.2), bins = c(0, 24, 48, 9999)) ruv residual error variability magnitude, specified list. dictionary data dictionary. Optional, named character vector specifies column names used dataset. group name column data groups observations together iterative flow. default observation "group", can used group peaks troughs together, group observations day together. Grouping done prior running analysis, changed afterwards. weights vector weights error. Length vector length observation vector. NULL (default), weights equal. Used MAP NP methods. Note `weights` argument also affect residuals (residuals scaled ). weight_prior weighting priors relationship observed data, default = 1 censor_covariates proseval tool PsN, “data leakage” (future covariates data): since NONMEM dataset step contains covariates future, technically data leakage, result -optimistic estimate predictive performance. mipdeval, covariate censoring future covariate data switched default (data leakage), can switched want match behavior PsN::proseval exactly. incremental MAP Bayesian incremental fits iterative loop? .e. case use first iterations MAP Bayesian estimates input second iteration, forth. uncertainty around MAP estimates used new omega matrix. approach called "model predictive control (MPC)" (www.page-meeting.org/?abstract=9076) may predictive "regular" MAP scenarios. Default FALSE. .stats_summ_options Options summary statistics. must result call stats_summ_options(). .vpc_options Options VPC simulations. must result call vpc_options(). .fit_options Options controlling MAP Bayesian fit. must result call fit_options(). threads number threads divide computations . Default 1, .e. parallel execution progress progress bar shown? Default TRUE, debugging package useful , since progress bar handlers obscure R output. verbose show output

title: Run iterative predictive analysis, looping over each individual's data — run_eval
text: mipdeval_results object, named list following elements: results: tibble one row per (iterative) prediction, holding identifiers (id, _iteration, _grouper, t), observation (dv), population predictions residuals (pred, res, wres, cwres), individual predictions residuals (iter_ipred, map_ipred, ires, iwres), objective function value (ofv), weighted sum--squares (ss_w), apriori flag, one column per model parameter, two families eta (random-effect) columns: iterative eta<nn> full-data map_eta<nn> estimates (see Details). mod_obj: parsed model object (see parse_model()): named list model information, including model, parameters, omega, ruv, fixed, bins, kappa. data: input data reading validation (see read_input_data() check_input_data()), data frame NONMEM-style records used analysis. sim: Simulated data used visual predictive check (VPC) NPDE, NULL simulations skipped (vpc_options(skip = TRUE)). stats_summ: tibble summarising forecasting performance statistics (see calculate_stats()). shrinkage: tibble eta-shrinkage per iteration (see calculate_shrinkage()). bayesian_impact: tibble Bayesian-impact values based RMSE MAPE (see calculate_bayesian_impact()). stats_summ, shrinkage, bayesian_impact NULL predictions produced (e.g. vpc_options(vpc_only = TRUE)).

title: Run iterative predictive analysis, looping over each individual's data — run_eval
text: run_eval() evaluates predictive performance way model used model-informed precision dosing (MIPD): subject walks observations time order , step, refits individual using data available point predict next observation(s). produces priori (population) prediction followed progressively informed posteriori (forecasting) predictions, mirroring iterative flow PsN's proseval tool. design results tibble reports quantities three levels individualization, easy confuse. predictions: pred: population prediction (typical individual, using individual-level data)—priori prediction. iter_ipred: iterative ("forecasting") individual prediction, using data available point—posteriori prediction. priori rows falls back pred. map_ipred: full-data MAP individual prediction, single retrospective fit subject's data . random-effect (eta) estimates follow structure: eta<nn> columns iterative ("forecasting") empirical Bayes estimates pair iter_ipred; evolve rows 0 priori (population) rows. map_eta<nn> columns full-data MAP empirical Bayes estimates pair map_ipred. constant per subject appear every row, including priori row. equivalent etas reported NONMEM output table, want empirical eta-distribution plot (one value per subject).

title: Core iterative simulation and MAP estimation function that loops over an individual's dataset — run_eval_core
text: mod_obj list object model information data NONMEM-style data.frame, path CSV file NONMEM data weights vector weights error. Length vector length observation vector. NULL (default), weights equal. Used MAP NP methods. Note `weights` argument also affect residuals (residuals scaled ). weight_prior weighting priors relationship observed data, default = 1 censor_covariates proseval tool PsN, “data leakage” (future covariates data): since NONMEM dataset step contains covariates future, technically data leakage, result -optimistic estimate predictive performance. mipdeval, covariate censoring future covariate data switched default (data leakage), can switched want match behavior PsN::proseval exactly. incremental MAP Bayesian incremental fits iterative loop? .e. case use first iterations MAP Bayesian estimates input second iteration, forth. uncertainty around MAP estimates used new omega matrix. approach called "model predictive control (MPC)" (www.page-meeting.org/?abstract=9076) may predictive "regular" MAP scenarios. Default FALSE. progress_function function increment progress bar .fit_options Options controlling MAP Bayesian fit. must result call fit_options().

title: mipdeval (development version)
text: Added support bootstrapping error metrics forecasted data: run_eval() now returns bootstrap_summ element point 95% confidence interval estimates error metric (e.g., RMSE, MPE, etc.). Bootstrapping configured new bootstrap argument stats_summ_options() (see bootstrap_options(); default). lower-level bootstrap_metrics() summarise_bootstrap_metrics() functions, well calculate_bootstrap_summ(), also exported general use. (#47) Added accuracy() statistic calculating proportion predicted drug concentrations fall within specified absolute relative error margins. statistic calculated run_eval() absolute relative error margins specified .stats_summ_options argument (see stats_summ_options()). (#30)

title: mipdeval 0.1.0
text: Initial version.
